Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class Apple2Font

Namespace
Pandowdy.UI
Assembly
Pandowdy.UI.dll

Apple II Enhanced Video ROM font data. Contains the character bitmaps for the Apple II 40/80 column text modes. Source: a2e_enh_video.rom (4096 bytes, but using first 2048 bytes)

The ROM file contains two font sets (4096 bytes total):

  • First half (0-2047): 256 characters × 8 bytes = 2048 bytes (used)
  • Second half (2048-4095): 256 characters × 8 bytes = 2048 bytes (reserved)
public static class Apple2Font
Inheritance
object
Apple2Font
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)

object.MemberwiseClone()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Fields

FontData

Apple II Enhanced Video ROM font data (2048 bytes). Each character occupies 8 bytes of bitmap data (7 pixels wide, 8 pixels tall). 256 characters × 8 bytes = 2048 bytes total.

public static readonly byte[] FontData

Field Value

byte[]

Methods

GetCharacterBitmap(byte)

Gets the font bitmap data for a specific character.

public static byte[] GetCharacterBitmap(byte charCode)

Parameters

charCode byte

ASCII character code (0-255)

Returns

byte[]

8-byte array containing the character bitmap (7 pixels wide, 8 pixels tall)

Remarks

Each byte represents one row of the character, with bits 0-6 representing the 7 pixels (bit 0 = leftmost pixel, bit 6 = rightmost pixel). Bit 7 is unused.

GetCharacterRow(byte, int)

Gets a single row of pixels for a character.

public static byte GetCharacterRow(byte charCode, int row)

Parameters

charCode byte

ASCII character code (0-255)

row int

Row index (0-7, where 0 is top row)

Returns

byte

Byte representing 7 pixels (bits 0-6, where bit 0 is leftmost)

Remarks

This is more efficient than GetCharacterBitmap(byte) when only a single row is needed for rendering or analysis.

Bit Layout: Bit 0 (LSB) = leftmost pixel, Bit 6 = rightmost pixel, Bit 7 = unused.

Exceptions

ArgumentOutOfRangeException

Thrown if row is not in range 0-7.
